data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: BA, WMT, NOC, SNOW
data.description:
Recent analyst actions include Snowflake (SNOW) receiving a rare double upgrade from Gary Alexander, who sees the recent pullback as an excellent entry point. Northrop Grumman (NOC) also earned an upgrade on prospects for substantially increased defense funding under a potential $1.5 trillion budget. On the downgrade side, Cash Flow Venue has moved Walmart (WMT) to Strong Sell, arguing the retailer’s valuation has become disconnected from its modest growth profile, while Kenio Fontes shifted Boeing (BA) to Hold, citing execution risks that outweigh the recovery narrative.

Upgrades

  • Snowflake (SNOW): Double Upgrade Sell to Buy by Gary Alexander. The analyst sees the recent ~20% pullback from October highs as a buying opportunity, pointing to enormous growth in remaining performance obligations (RPO) and strong AI-driven customer demand as indicators of long-term revenue visibility.

    “What makes Snowflake more appealing today is the fact that while its valuation has moderated over the past quarter, many of its peers in the 20%-30% growth software bucket have seen meaningful expansion in multiples as the stock market rallied into 2025's year-end. Snowflake still sits at the higher end of its peer multiples, but given its RPO growth and potential acceleration in revenue, I find now to be an excellent entry point.”

  • Northrop Grumman (NOC): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Dhierin Bechai. The analyst believes a substantially higher defense budget would de-risk funding for the F/A-XX program and that Northrop Grumman is strategically positioned to win the contract given freed engineering resources post-B-21.

    “Having a substantially bigger defense budget would substantially increase the chances for the F/A-XX to receive appropriate funding, giving the program a more credible and committed development path. … I also believe that in order to maintain a diverse and competitive industry, Northrop Grumman is the best choice to develop the next generation fighter jet for the US Navy.”

Downgrades

  • Walmart (WMT): Downgrade Hold to Strong Sell by Cash Flow Venue. Despite solid business performance, the analyst argues Walmart’s nearly 45x forward P/E multiple is unjustifiable for a company with low-to-mid single-digit revenue growth, especially compared to faster-growing tech giants trading at lower multiples.

    “Walmart's valuation is quite high, as it trades at nearly 45x forward-looking P/E ratio, which I view as more common across businesses growing much faster. At first sight, some could consider WMT's valuation reasonable given Costco's valuation (COST), but I don't see that to be the case.”

  • The Boeing Company (BA): Downgrade Buy to Hold by Kenio Fontes. The analyst cautions that despite Boeing’s structural importance to aviation and defense, commercial aircraft execution challenges and legacy contract issues create a risk-reward profile that isn’t compelling at current valuations.

    “Putting all this together with the extremely cloudy scenario, it seems to me that Boeing’s case is still not that attractive. There is enormous complexity, and even counting on normalization, the asymmetry does not seem sufficient to justify all the risks and uncertainties of the case.”

data.title: BlackRock Q4 Earnings Preview: What to expect
data.description:
BlackRock (BLK) is set to announce fourth-quarter earnings on Thursday, and investors will focus on the company’s organic base fee growth, 2026 guidance, as well as its core general and administrative (G&A) expenses and buybacks.

Wall Street expects the global asset manager to post EPS of $12.21, implying a 2.8% year-over-year increase, while revenue is expected to rise 17.4% to $6.67B during the quarter.

The asset manager, during its Q3 earnings call, stated that it expects a low teens percentage increase in 2025 core G&A expense, mainly driven by the onboarding of GIP, Preqin, and HPS. It also added that it expects to repurchase at least $375M of shares in the fourth quarter.

UBS gave BlackRock a Nuetral rating but increased its price target to $1,218 from $1,180.

UBS analyst Michael Brown said that he expects base organic fee growth in the 7.5%-8.5% range for Q4 (UBS 7.8%)—above its 5%+ target. While 2026E guidance may not be a major catalyst, the market is increasingly recognizing BLK’s shift from a margin stabilization narrative to a margin expansion narrative, particularly on the expense side.

Seeking Alpha's Quant and analysts are cautious, recommending the stock a Hold, while the Wall Street analysts are bullish, rating the company a Buy.

Seeking Alpha author Scott Kaufman rated BLK a Buy, saying that BlackRock stands as the global asset management leader, managing nearly $13.5T in AUM and leveraging dominant ETF and software platforms.

“BLK targets 10% annual revenue growth, aiming to reach $35 billion by 2030, with adjusted operating margins around 45% and 12.2% EPS CAGR through 2027,” Kaufman added.

Over the past year, BLK shares gained nearly 14%, compared with a 19.3% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

Over the last year, BLK has beaten EPS estimates 100% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 50% of the time.

In the past three months, EPS estimates have seen three upward revisions versus seven downward moves, while revenue estimates have seen five upward revisions against two downward revisions.

data.title: Italian government set to re-appoint Eni CEO Descalzi - reports
data.description:
Italy's Prime Minister Meloni is preparing to support Eni (E) CEO Claudio Descalzi for a new three-year term to lead the state-backed company, according to multiple reports.

Descalzi has led Eni (E) since 2014, and a reappointment for a fifth term would make him the company's longest-serving head.

Meloni's ​administration, which controls Eni (E) with a nearly 32% stake, will call the shots at the company's shareholder meeting in May, when investors ‍are due to vote for the appointment of the management team.

Meloni reportedly also is expected to extend CEO mandates at other key Italian state-backed companies, including defense contractor Leonardo (FINMF) (FINMY) and energy company Enel (ENLAY), although talks over the appointments are ongoing and still in progress.

Analysts say a top challenge for Descalzi the next three years ‍will be to support growth at the company's new units and make sure that returns promised to ​investment partners are delivered.

data.title: Fed's Miran sees deregulation allowing policymakers to lower interest rates
data.description:
Federal Reserve Governor Stephen Miran said on Wednesday that if monetary policymakers ignore the deflationary effects of deregulation, then it can cause an unnecessary economic contraction.

"I believe that the sweeping deregulation underway in the United States will significantly boost competition, productivity, and potential growth, allowing faster economic growth without putting upward pressure on inflation," he said in a speech at the Delphi Economic Forum in Athens, Greece. "This would support continued easing of restrictive monetary policy, but ignoring these effects would result in monetary policy that is needlessly contractionary."

Regulations can have large consequences for supply, he said. 

"The primary effects of deregulation are on the supply side of the economy and have the effect of increasing potential output more than they increase actual output," Miran said. "If demand increases and production is limited by regulation or some other factor, then prices increase. If demand rises and these shackles have been removed, then output grows and pressure on prices is much smaller."

When actual output falls below potential output, there's slack in the economy that could be accommodated by looser monetary policy,  he added. "If deregulation boosts potential output above actual, the correct response is to cut rates."

Miran advises that central bankers pay close attention to the effects of regulation on productivity, output, and prices. "In recent quarters, policy has been tighter than it should have been to reflect significant deregulation lifting potential growth and reducing inflation," he said.

data.title: BARK rallies after Marcus Lemonis and GNK enter the bidding war for a takeover
data.description:
BARK (BARK) soared in early trading on Wednesday after the company attracted an offer from a group that includes GNK Holdings LLC and Marcus Lemonis at a higher price than the bid from Great Dane Ventures. GNK and Lemonis submitted a preliminary, non-binding indication of interest to acquire BARK (BARK) in an all-cash transaction valued at $1.10 per share. 

The proposed transaction would value BARK at an implied enterprise value of approximately $188.7 million, which was noted to represent a 22% premium to the previously announced proposal by Great Dane Ventures. 

The group believes BARK (BARK) represents a compelling opportunity to unlock value through disciplined execution, enhanced merchandising, and more in-depth customer engagement across its differentiated platform. "This proposal reflects our strong conviction in BARK’s brand, customer loyalty, and long-term potential," stated GNK President Nachum Klugman.

For his part, Lemonis is still the CEO of Bed Bath & Beyond (BBBY) but recently stepped down from the CEO job at Camping World (CWH).

Shares of BARK were up 20.7% at 10:42 a.m. and are more than 64% higher over the last week.

data.title: SLB in talks with U.S. officials, Chevron to expand in Venezuela - FT
data.description:
Oilfield services company SLB (SLB) is talking with Trump administration officials, Chevron (CVX) and other potential customers about expanding in Venezuela, part of President Trump's plan to revive Venezuela's crippled oil industry, Financial Times reported Wednesday.

SLB (SLB) has the longest and largest presence of any oilfield services operator in Venezuela, after navigating U.S. sanctions and expropriations that led rivals to exit the country; while the company downsized its presence in 2016 and took a $983M impairment a year later, it remained a major service provider to Chevron (CVX), the only U.S. oil producer still operating in Venezuela.

While SLB (SLB) has a licence to operate with Venezuela's state-owned oil company PDVSA until May 2025, the bulk of its work is through Chevron's licence, according to the report.

Investors see SLB (SLB) and other oil services companies as best positioned to benefit from Trump's effort to cajole operators to invest $100B to rebuild Venezuela’s oil sector, and SLB shares have jumped 14% since U.S. forces captured Venezuela's former leader Maduro on January 3; Chevron (CVX) has gained 5%, while rival oilfield services companies Halliburton (HAL) and Baker Hughes (BKR) have added 10% and 4%, respectively.

Baker Hughes (BKR) has some employees in Venezuela to support power and gas services for
Chevron (CVX), but significantly reduced its presence when U.S. sanctions were tightened in 2019, and Halliburton (HAL) left Venezuela that year but told FT it could move quickly to re-enter the country.

data.title: Intuitive Surgical falls as 2026 da Vinci growth projection misses expectations
data.description:
• Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) is off ~5% in Wednesday morning trading after issuing 2026 guidance for growth of its Da Vinci surgical system that fell short of Street expectations.
  • The company is projecting growth of 13%-15%. A Bloomberg consensus estimate is 15.2%.
  • Intuitive Surgical said that on a preliminary basis, 2025 da Vinci growth was ~18% and overall 2025 revenue was $10.06B. Consensus is $9.93B.
  • Preliminary Q4 revenue is $2.87B. Consensus is $2.72B.
  • Intuitive Surgical is scheduled to report Q4 and full-year financial results on Jan. 22.

data.title: Mid-to-Low cap consumer staples stocks facing poor EPS revision grades
data.description:
As the earnings week begins, a review of mid to low cap consumer staples stocks reveals several companies currently receiving the weakest grades based on analyst earnings revisions.

This analysis focuses on identifying stocks where analysts have significantly lowered their earnings per share expectations.

The metric used for this assessment is the EPS Revisions Grade, which reflects the direction and magnitude of analyst estimate changes. The following list contains consumer staples companies that have received grades of ‘F’ or ‘D-’, indicating substantial downward revisions to earnings expectations.

Here are the consumer staples stocks with the lowest EPS Revisions Grades:

  1. Sendas Distribuidora S.A. (ASAIY) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  2. Edgewell Personal Care (EPC) — EPS Revisions Grade: F 
  3. Flowers Foods (FLO) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  4. Premium Brands (PRBZF) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  5. PriceSmart (PSMT) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  6. Shiseido Company (SSDOY) — EPS Revisions Grade: F 
  7. USANA Health Sciences, Inc. (USNA) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  8. Westrock Coffee Company (WEST) — EPS Revisions Grade: F
  9. BellRing Brands, Inc. (BRBR) — EPS Revisions Grade: D-
  10. Barry Callebaut AG (BRRLY) — EPS Revisions Grade: D-

Consumer Staples ETFs: (XLP), (VDC), (IYK), (FSTA), (KXI), and (RSPS)

data.title: UK Prime Minister Starmer says X working to comply with UK law on AI deepfakes
data.description:
British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Wednesday that the X social network is working to comply with British law pertaining to artificial intelligence deepfake images. 

“I have been informed this morning that X is acting to ensure full compliance with UK law,” Starmer told parliament, according to Reuters. 

Formerly known as Twitter, X merged with xAI (X.AI) last year. Elon Musk controls both companies.

The U.K. is set to bring into force a law — perhaps as soon as this week — that will make it illegal for companies to provide tools for artificial intelligence deepfakes, according to previous reporting. Technology Secretary Liz Kendall told the House of Commons that the law would prevent the tools from being released. She also said the AI deepfakes were not “harmless,” but rather “weapons of abuse.”

Concerns have risen recently in the country regarding x.AI's Grok chatbot, following a growing outcry over its use for sexualized deepfakes of women and children. On Friday, xAI said it restricted the use of AI service Grok's image generation features to paying subscribers.

In response to the outrage, Musk took to X on Jan. 3 and wrote that “anyone using Grok to make illegal content will suffer the same consequences as if they upload illegal content.”

On Monday, the U.K. online safety watchdog Ofcom launched an investigation into Musk's X to determine if the company complied with its duties to protect people in the U.K. from content that is illegal in the country.

data.title: Fed's Paulson says she still sees room for more rate cuts
data.description:
Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Anna Paulson said Wednesday she still feels "cautiously optimistic on inflation" after the latest data, reiterating her view for additional interest-rate cuts later this year.

“I am feeling cautiously optimistic on inflation, and I see a decent chance that we will end the year with inflation that is close to 2% on a run-rate basis,” Paulson said in a prepared speech at an event with the Chamber of Commerce for Greater Philadelphia.

Her overall 2026 economic outlook includes inflation moderating, the labor market stabilizing and around 2% growth. "If all of that happens, then some modest further adjustments to the funds rate would likely be appropriate later in the year,” Paulson said.

On Tuesday, the Bureau of Labor Statistics published the Consumer Price Index report, which featured slightly softer-than-expected core CPI at +2.6% Y/Y in December. Meanwhile, November wholesale inflation data out Wednesday heated up a bit.

Paulson said she will keep a close eye on labor-market developments, as job-market "risks have risen and that has been an important factor in my support for the 75 basis points of cuts that the FOMC did last year. The median Fed official expects only one 25-basis-point reduction this year.
